name: Security Service Federal Credit Union – Headquarters
description_short: Member-owned federal credit union offering checking, savings, certificates, mortgages, and auto loans with competitive rates and insurance discounts.
description_long:
Security Service Federal Credit Union (SSFCU) is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution operating under routing number 314088637. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, SSFCU structures itself around member benefit rather than shareholder profit, which the organization emphasizes as a fundamental operational difference from commercial banks.

SSFCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (7-month at 3.85% APY, 15-month at 3.75% APY), mortgages (15-year fixed at 5.503% APR, 30-year fixed at 6.157% APR), auto loans, online and mobile banking, bill payment services, and insurance products. The organization provides phone protection coverage up to $1,800 on certain checking accounts and advertises insurance savings up to $985 through real agents. Members can access 24/7 account management via Security Service Online Banking and mobile app.

SSFCU differentiates itself through member-exclusive benefits including free notary and signature guarantee services, free self-service coin counters, and discounted tax preparation. The organization emphasizes security and convenience through multiple payment channels including online banking, mobile app deposits, phone-based payments (1.866.397.4512), and integration with ACI Payments, Inc. The 2024 Annual Report is publicly available for member review.

As a credit union, SSFCU maintains standard financial institution limitations: membership eligibility requirements, modest marketing presence compared to major banks, and typical credit union operational constraints. The organization's mortgage and auto loan rates are competitive but not necessarily lowest-market. Insurance products are offered through partnership rather than direct provision. Mobile check deposit requires eligibility qualification.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Competitive certificate rates (3.85% APY on 7-month CDs, 3.75% on 15-month) with low $50k minimums
Free notary service, signature guarantees, and coin counting for members
Multiple payment channels: online banking, mobile app, phone, and third-party processors with no fees for mobile payments
Phone protection coverage up to $1,800 on Power Protected Checking with up to two claims annually
24/7 account access via mobile app including mobile check deposit capability
Insurance partnerships offering savings up to $985 with human agents
Member-owned structure with published annual reports and 2026 annual meeting transparency
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not fully detailed on website (typical for credit unions but may exclude some consumers)
Mortgage rates (5.503%-6.157%) not definitively lower than competitive market alternatives
Mobile check deposit subject to 'eligibility and qualification requirements' with unclear approval criteria
Insurance products sold through partnerships rather than direct offering, potentially limiting customization
Limited information about account minimums, overdraft fees, or account maintenance costs on homepage
best_for: Credit union members seeking competitive savings rates on certificates of deposit with lower minimums
Homebuyers and auto loan seekers comparing member-owned credit union mortgage and auto loan rates
Consumers prioritizing free financial services like notary and signature guarantees
Mobile-first banking customers needing remote check deposit and 24/7 account access
diagnosis: SSFCU is best for existing or eligible members seeking competitive rates on deposits and loans combined with member-exclusive service benefits like free notaries and coin counting. Main caveat: credit union membership eligibility requirements and the lack of published details about account fees, minimums, and service terms on the homepage require direct inquiry before enrollment.
services: Checking accounts with phone protection coverage
Savings accounts and certificates of deposit
Online banking with recurring payment setup
Mobile banking app with remote check deposit
Phone-based payment scheduling and recurring payments
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
